posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked: 1996 Kalin Cellars Sémillon, Livermore Valley. I’ve been on a Kalin kick lately…which is an awesome kick to be on, ha! This Sémillon was outstanding! Easily the best domestic example I’ve ever had, and right up there with the top Hunter Valley offerings. A nose full of bright citrus and a stunning, medium bodied palate with lemon curd, lime, and lanolin and with VERY long and slightly bitter finish (in a good way). Unbelievably fresh, delicious, and contemplative all at the same time. What a privilege and a treat to drink! #kalincellars for the win yet again!

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked:
Had the 96 “Reserve” years ago…thought it was oxed, but totally not! Drank like a fine aged Bord Blanc! Kalin shocks…and rocks!

Oh shit…I’m talking SB not Semillon. Love the sem too! Waxy and tropical!

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
B_Buzzini:

Had the 96 “Reserve” years ago…thought it was oxed, but totally not! Drank like a fine aged Bord Blanc! Kalin shocks…and rocks!

Oh shit…I’m talking SB not Semillon. Love the sem too! Waxy and tropical!

I mean…can you go wrong either way?? Ha!

And agreed! Absolutely tropical and waxy! Almost a chenin-like thing going on but more lime/citrus/bitter notes. So good!

posts.name: Brian Buzzini
posts.cooked: Looks like a couple 94’s left in the cellar.

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
B_Buzzini:

Looks like a couple 94’s left in the cellar.

One can only hope for a Buzz note on that wine!

posts.name: Matt Wood
posts.cooked: Nice note Rich. I have one more of those hanging around. We had a 1996 Cline Semillon that was amazing the other day. I think the Kalin Semillon wines were made from the old vine Semillon vineyard that Wente owned, planted with cuttings from chateau d’yquem back in the day. Harbor winery made some Semillon from there too.

posts.name: Thomas Keim
posts.cooked: Kalin’s Semillon was always my favorite offering from them. Thanks for the note Rich -

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
Matt_Wood:

Nice note Rich. I have one more of those hanging around. We had a 1996 Cline Semillon that was amazing the other day. I think the Kalin Semillon wines were made from the old vine Semillon vineyard that Wente owned, planted with cuttings from chateau d’yquem back in the day. Harbor winery made some Semillon from there too.

That’s awesome Matt! Had no idea on the vineyard source so i really appreciate the background!

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
Thomas_Keim:

Kalin’s Semillon was always my favorite offering from them. Thanks for the note Rich -

I have to agree! The sparkling rose the other day was incredible, but im loving the Sémillon a bit more than the chards ive had. I believe they made a chenin one year and im trying to get my hands on one of those!

posts.name: Brian Pusser
posts.cooked:
Here’s a link to a thoughtful World of Fine Wine tribute to Kalin, written after the passing of co-founder Terry Leighton:

World Of Fine Wine – 9 Mar 23

Terrance James Leighton (1944–2023): High roads less traveled at Kalin Cellars

William Kelley pays tribute to Terry Leighton, the self-effacing genius behind Kalin Cellars, California’s most idiosyncratic winery.

Est. reading time: 5 minutes

posts.name: Rich Brown
posts.cooked:
Brian_P1:

Here’s a link to a thoughtful World of Fine Wine tribute to Kalin, written after the passing of co-founder Terry Leighton:

https://worldoffinewine.com/news-features/kalin-cellars-terry-leighton

That…was a GREAT read Brian! Thank you so much for sharing it!

And definitely props to @William_Kelley for writing it

posts.name: James Sanders
posts.cooked: Thank you for posting this, Brian.

posts.name: Clayton Wai-Poi
posts.cooked: Anyone know which vineyard in Livermore this is sourced from?

posts.name: Matt Wood
posts.cooked: I don’t think the vineyard is there anymore or at least the quantity of semillon from Livermore has slowed a lot. Harbor and LVC used to make wine from there also. Wente owned it but I think it was on the old Charles Wetmore/ Cresta Blanca property they had purchased. I think it was originally planted in the 1880’s with cuttings from y’quem. The early Cresta Blanca wines won lots of awards. Hopefully someone else I’m not aware of is getting the grapes now. Uncle Yu’s restaurant in Livermore always has lots of Kalin wines on the list.

posts.name: Clayton Wai-Poi
posts.cooked:
Hmmm… perhaps the Wente owned Murrieta’s Well estate vineyard, that claims to have originally been planned by cuttings from Yquem and Chateau Margaux?

Murrieta’s Well

Our Story - Murrieta’s Well

The winery known today as Murrieta’s Well has a long history in California’s Livermore Valley. This is the timeline of our story.

(they also used to bottle a Sauvignon/Semillon, although I don’t see anything too recent in cellartrtacker)

posts.name: Clayton Wai-Poi
posts.cooked: Although further snooping would suggest the Cresta Blanca property is where the current Wente tasting room is, so could be either? Not sure they’ve ripped up the vines though.

posts.name: Clayton Wai-Poi
posts.cooked: And a final post after googling it… general observations seems to suggest its the 1880s vines at Wente (i.e. ex Cresta Blanca),

posts.name: Matt Wood
posts.cooked:
Nice finds. Murrietas Well does have a Wente connection. I do think the semillon comes from the Cresta Blanca vines. That is the first recognized imported Semillon to the US.
Quote from the fps website —-
“Wetmore is relevant to the grapevine collection at Foundation Plant Services because he was responsible for bringing to California from France in the early 1880’s the original source material for Sauvignon blanc FPS 01 and for Sémillon FPS 02. Although the story will be told in greater detail below in connection with Sauvignon blanc 01, Wetmore travelled to Bordeaux with a letter of introduction to the owner of Château d’Yquem and returned to California with cuttings of Sauvignon blanc, Sémillon and Muscadelle.”—

It would be awesome if some started producing Semillon again from that vineyard. I do still have 1 bottle of 2006 Livermore Valley Cellars made from there and went through a bunch of the 1989 Harbor Semillon when they were being sold pretty cheap at Corti a few years ago.

posts.name: Matt Wood
posts.cooked:
I dug a little more and it looks like Louis Mel who owned the winery and vineyards that are now Murrieta’s Well (and are now owned by Wente) provided the letter of introduction for Wetmore to the owners of Yquem. Mel’s wife was friends with the owners. Below is the location of where Cresta Blanca was located, you can see the white cliffs that sat above it in the large bend of Arroyo Rd.
Cresta Blanca location

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: That’s correct! Cuttings taken by Charles Wetmore.
